title: Powerhouse React hooks from @powerhousedao/reactor-browser derive all data from the Reactor and return document-dispatch tuples for mutation
content: The `@powerhousedao/reactor-browser` package provides dozens of React hooks organized into categories: selected document, document by ID, document cache, drives, nodes/folders, items in drive/folder, node actions, modals, revision history, timeline, and config. The core pattern is a `[document, dispatch]` tuple where dispatch has the signature `(actionOrActions: Action | Action[] | undefined, onErrors?: (errors: Error[]) => void) => void`. All hook data ultimately derives from the Reactor, which manages asynchronous eventually-consistent state of drives and documents. Suspense-aware variants like `useDocument` suspend rendering while loading, while `useGetDocumentAsync` returns status/data/error without suspending.
description: Hooks like useSelectedDocument return [document, dispatch] tuples where dispatch accepts actions or action arrays with optional error callbacks.
